title: Symphyotrichum × schistosum
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum × schistosum is classified as a hybrid species within the genus Symphyotrichum. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The exact classification of Symphyotrichum × schistosum is as a cross between Symphyotrichum novi-belgii and Symphyotrichum pilosum. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup. As a hybrid, Symphyotrichum × schistosum exhibits traits from both parent species.
uses: Symphyotrichum × schistosum, also known as the Shasta daisy, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds that have been shown to have potential therapeutic effects. intro: Symphyotrichum × schistosum, also known as the hybrid ironweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Symphyotrichum × schistosum is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as in the form of tinctures and infusions, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
